summary: Large first-pass decompilation of Link's boomerang item code. The PR replaced many placeholder stubs in src/melee/it/items/itlinkboomerang.c with C implementations, added Link boomerang item-variable and attribute structs, improved boomerang prototypes, connected Item.xDD4_itemVar.linkboomerang into the item union, and adjusted Link's boomerang removal helper to return the result of ftLk_Init_BoomerangExists. The decomp.dev report credited 16 newly matched itlinkboomerang functions and 16 additional partially matched functions, for +1788 matched bytes.

key_files.role: Main decompilation target; replaced placeholder markers with roughly 780 lines of Link boomerang item logic, helpers, math routines, animation/display handling, cleanup, collision response, and state callbacks.
Added itLinkBoomerang_ItemVars and itLinkBoomerangAttributes with offset-based fields and padding for the large boomerang item-var region.
Replaced many UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS boomerang declarations with concrete Item_GObj, Fighter_GObj, Vec3, Fighter_Part, bool, void, and f32 signatures.
Added linkboomerang to Item.xDD4_itemVar union and refined ItemStateDesc.x8_parameters from UNK_T to HSD_ShapeAnimJoint*.
Changed ftLk_SpecialS_RemoveBoomerang0 from void to bool and returned ftLk_Init_BoomerangExists after clearing Link's boomerang state.
Updated the ftLk_SpecialS_RemoveBoomerang0 prototype to match its recovered bool return type.

what_changed: Implemented concrete C bodies for many Link boomerang functions that had previously been placeholder markers, including fully matched functions reported by decomp.dev such as it_8029FDBC, it_8029FDDC, it_802A07B4, it_802A0810, it_802A087C, it_802A0E70, it_802A0F08, it_802A0F88, it_802A0FB0, it_802A12DC, it_802A1948, it_802A1F08, it_802A1FA8, it_802A2048, it_802A2288, and it_802A23CC.

Made partial matching progress on larger boomerang functions including it_8029FE64, it_802A0534, it_802A0930, it_802A0C34, it_802A0FD0, it_802A10E4, it_802A13EC, it_802A15EC, it_802A16E4, it_802A19E0, it_802A1C30, it_802A1D60, it_802A20E8, and it_802A2320.
Added local math helpers and macros in itlinkboomerang.c, including M_TAU, DEG2RAD, my_sqrtf using __frsqrte refinement, HYPOT, VEC_XY_LENGTH, clamp_angle_pi, clamp_tau, clamp_pi_tau, and norm_xF74.
Added remove_boomerang helper to clear the owning Link fighter's boomerang state only when the item owner matches xDD4_itemVar.linkboomerang.xF98 and xDE8 is not 1.

Added boomerang item animation/JObj handling for xF90[2], including display of extra JObjs, removal via HSD_JObjRemoveAll, animation updates, rotation preservation, and repeated lb_8000BA0C scaling/application over the child and xF90 objects.

Added collision and trajectory logic using lbVector_CosAngle, lbVector_Mirror, atan2f, sinf, cosf, velocity length adjustment, wall/ceiling/floor normals, and return-to-owner steering through ftLk_SpecialHi_GetPosWithAdjustedY.
Introduced itLinkBoomerang_ItemVars with known offsets from ip+DD4 through ip+F98 and itLinkBoomerangAttributes with f32 fields x0 through x40 plus HSD_Joint* x44 and x48.
Changed ftLk_SpecialS_RemoveBoomerang0 to return bool instead of void, preserving the state-clearing side effects while returning ftLk_Init_BoomerangExists(gobj).
Refined itlinkboomerang.h prototypes away from generic unknown signatures, making many item callbacks callable with typed arguments.
The author explicitly noted this was a first real decompilation attempt and that most functions were 80-100% matched, while it_802A013C remained difficult due to a large run of zeroing.
smart_moves: Factored repeated angle normalization into small helpers such as clamp_tau, clamp_pi_tau, clamp_angle_pi, and norm_xF74, which made the boomerang's direction and facing-dir adjustments reusable across velocity, collision, and return-to-owner routines.
Used a custom my_sqrtf with __frsqrte refinement and a volatile float result to approximate the compiler/codegen behavior needed for hypot-style vector-length calculations.

Introduced no-inline wrapper functions such as it_8029FE64_no_inline, it_802A15EC_no_inline, and it_802A19E0_no_inline, a common matching tactic when the original compiler's inlining decisions need to be controlled.
Preserved child JObj rotation around calls that change item state/animation, e.g. reading a Quaternion before Item_80268E5C or collision updates and restoring it afterward.
Recovered enough of the item-var layout to replace raw offsets in many places with ip->xDD4_itemVar.linkboomerang fields, while still using M2C_FIELD for unresolved Vec/array regions.
Kept uncertain regions explicit with TODO comments and M2C_FIELD accesses rather than over-naming unknown arrays or pretending complete struct knowledge.
decomp_lessons: A partially recovered item-var struct can still unlock many matches: adding the xDD4_itemVar.linkboomerang union member allowed most logic to use structured fields while leaving unresolved gaps padded or accessed through M2C_FIELD.

When item behavior is tightly coupled to a fighter, confirm fighter helper return types; here ftLk_SpecialS_RemoveBoomerang0 needed to return the bool result of ftLk_Init_BoomerangExists rather than discard it.
For projectile/returning-item work, expect repeated angle wrapping around both pi and tau; helper functions reduce duplicated branching and improve matchability.
JObj animation callbacks may require preserving rotations across state transitions, collision updates, and HSD_JObj animation calls to match original side effects.
Large decomp PRs can still be useful when split between exact matches and documented partials; decomp.dev's report clearly separated 16 new 100% matches from 16 incomplete but improved functions.
Do not prematurely name unknown attribute fields; this PR kept boomerang attributes as x0, x4, etc. while recovering their type and layout, which is safer until semantics are proven.
Array-like trailing item-var regions may need padding plus raw offset access until their exact Vec3 or table shape is understood; the PR specifically called out a likely Vec3[] field as TODO.
naming_conventions: New structs used subsystem-prefixed names: itLinkBoomerang_ItemVars and itLinkBoomerangAttributes.
Unidentified item-var and attribute fields retained offset names such as xDD4, xDE8, xF74, xF90, xF98, and x0 through x48.
The Item union member name followed lowercase article style: xDD4_itemVar.linkboomerang.
Function names remained address-based it_802A.... symbols because semantic names were not yet available.
Helper names were descriptive but local/static where appropriate: remove_boomerang, norm_xF74, clamp_tau, clamp_pi_tau, loop_lb_8000BA0C.
Known placeholder/uncertainty conventions were preserved with TODO comments and M2C_FIELD rather than invented semantic names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used m2c_macros.h and M2C_FIELD for fields/arrays whose struct layout was not yet fully recovered, including accesses around offsets 0xDDC, 0xDF0, and 0xEB0.
Added PAD_STACK in functions where stack shape likely mattered for matching, such as it_802A0E70, it_802A10E4, and it_802A16E4.
Used no-inline wrappers to influence call/inlining shape for matching.
Implemented square root through __frsqrte Newton refinement rather than plain sqrtf, likely to reproduce PowerPC floating-point code generation.
Used exact constants/macros such as M_TAU, M_PI, DEG2RAD, 0x2715B, 0x111CB, and ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE where the decompiled logic needed literal behavior.
Retained unresolved it_802A013C as a placeholder; the PR body specifically identified it as hard due to a large zeroing run.

review_feedback.feedback: Author noted weak areas: this was their first real decompilation attempt, most functions were only 80-100% matched, and it_802A013C was not solved because a huge zeroing sequence stumped them.
Automated report showed +0.05% matched code and +1788 bytes for GALE01, with 16 new 100% function matches and 16 additional improvements in unmatched itlinkboomerang functions.
No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish or rename it_802A013C in src/melee/it/items/itlinkboomerang.c.
Search for later changes replacing M2C_FIELD accesses in Link boomerang with a real Vec3 array layout.
Search for semantic names assigned to itLinkBoomerangAttributes fields x0 through x48.
Look for future review or cleanup of the TODO angle initialization in it_802A0534.
Check whether ftLk_SpecialS_RemoveBoomerang0 returning bool affected other Link boomerang or SpecialS callers.
